name: BRANCHIAL CLEFT ANOMALIES
definition: BRANCHIAL CLEFT CYST failure of involution of branchial cleft PAROTID LYMPHOEPITHELIAL CYST incomplete obliteration of 2nd branchial cleft tract (cervical sinus of His) resulting in sinus above superior laryngeal nerve below superior laryngeal nerve

demographics.Frequency: 2nd most common congenital lesion of head & neck after thyroglossal duct cyst!
demographics.Location: from submandibular triangle (posterior / inferior to angle of mandible) near
demographics.Path: 1-10-cm large thin-walled cyst, lined by stratified squamous epithelium overlying

dominant_finding: well-marginated thin-walled ovoid compressible hypoechoic mass:
distribution: from submandibular triangle (posterior / inferior to angle of mandible) near
discriminating_features: performed for localization of a fistulous tract in 2nd + 3rd arch anomalies
cystic mass within parotid or immediate periparotid region (superficial to / deep to parotid
may extend into adjacent fat-containing parapharyngeal space ± connection to EAC
oval / round cyst near mandibular angle
displacement of sternocleidomastoid muscle posteriorly, carotid artery + jugular vein

general: performed for localization of a fistulous tract in 2nd + 3rd arch anomalies
cystic mass within parotid or immediate periparotid region (superficial to / deep to parotid
may extend into adjacent fat-containing parapharyngeal space ± connection to EAC
oval / round cyst near mandibular angle
displacement of sternocleidomastoid muscle posteriorly, carotid artery + jugular vein
cyst may enlarge after upper respiratory tract infection / injury
US: well-marginated thin-walled ovoid compressible hypoechoic mass:
occasionally fine internal echoes = internal debris (due to hemorrhage / infection)
"beak" sign = curved rim of tissue pointing medially between internal + external carotid
cystic mass projecting from deep margin of faucial tonsil toward skull base
unilocular cystic mass within posterior cervical space
